Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, 1927-2013
Collection Number: 4254

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, 1927-2013
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
4254
Abstract:
Papers of Dr. Joyce Brothers include correspondence, subject files, letters received, scripts, clippings, contracts, awards, photographs, films, videos, and articles and books by Dr. Brothers.
Creator:
Brothers, Joyce.
Quanitities:
300 cubic feet.
Boxes 63-67 are closed for 90 years from date of creation.
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. You may request to have view the digitized version, or request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized is noted in the finding aid. This restriction covers all film, all video, all tape recordings, and AV material in boxes 193-229, 240-244, and 250-251.
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Joyce Diane Brothers (October 20, 1927 – May 13, 2013) was an American psychologist, television personality, advice columnist, and writer. She graduated from Cornell University in 1947, and with a PhD from Columbia University in 1953.
She first became famous in 1955 for winning the top prize on the American game show "The $64,000 Question." Her fame from the game show allowed her to go on to host various advice columns and television shows, which established her as a pioneer in the field of "pop (popular) psychology".
Brothers is often credited as the first to normalize psychological concepts to the American mainstream. Her syndicated columns were featured in newspapers and magazines, including a monthly column for Good Housekeeping, in which she contributed for nearly 40 years. Her television programs, where she read letters from viewers and offered advice, began in 1958.
As Brothers quickly became the "face of psychology" for American audiences, she often appeared in various television roles, usually as herself. From the 1970s onward, she also began to accept fictional roles that parodied her "woman psychologist" persona. She is noted for working continuously for five decades across various genres.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, #4254. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Papers of Dr. Joyce Brothers include correspondence, subject files, scripts, clippings, contracts, awards, photographs, video, film, tape recordings, and books documenting the over 50 year career of Dr. Joyce Brothers.
CUSTODIAL HISTORY

The first donation was made in 1986 by Dr. Brothers, with subsequent donations being made yearly. Upon her death in 2013, the rest of her material, including her filing cabinets of research and articles, was donated by her family. Thirteen filing cabinets containing notes, articles, and other documents, were badly damaged by storage conditions and never accessioned by Cornell. That material was destroyed in 2020.
An additional 5 boxes of material was retrieved from the Brothers' farm house in 2023.
CUSTODIAL HISTORY

The first donation was made in 1986 by Dr. Brothers, with subsequent donations being made yearly. Upon her death in 2013, the rest of her material, including her filing cabinets of research and articles, was donated by her family. Thirteen filing cabinets containing notes, articles, and other documents, were badly damaged by storage conditions and never accessioned by Cornell. That material was destroyed in 2020.
An additional 5 boxes of material was retrieved from the Brothers' farm house in 2023.

Series IV. Work Files
Subseries IVa. Alpha subject work files
Scope and Contents
The files in this series were part of the first accession, originally labelled "Selected files from JB."
Processing Information
Some titles may be supplied by previous archivists. Notes have been added to clarify contents of folders. Order has been maintained. Boxes 1-7 were labelled "Alpha Subject Work File." Boxes 8-12 were labelled "Farm File."

Subseries IVb. Alpha files
Scope and Contents
This subseries consists of material organized alphabetically by Brothers, but not part of subseries Ia.
Processing Information
The files in this subseries are labelled by Brothers unless otherwise noted. Some titles may be edited for clarity . Boxes 14, 15, and 16 were removed from filing cabinets and kept in their original order with original titles.

Subseries IVc. Non-alpha Work Files
Processing Information
These boxes were removed from filing cabinets. Boxes 14-17 were kept in their original order with original titles. Boxes 18-20 were compiled by the archivist from miscellaneous boxes and files. Some of the folder titles for those boxes were created by the archivist. The filing cabinets were originally labelled "Book publishing, media tours, promos, luce, book review, books-scripts, agents (various) #1"

Series VII. Television and Radio Programs by Brothers
Subseries VIIa. "Consult Dr. Brothers"
Scope and Contents
"Consult Dr. Brothers" changed titles and is also referred to as "The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show" or "The Dr. Joyce Brothers Program" in the early years. It is unclear when exactly the name changed, but was clearly "Consult Dr. Brothers" in 1961. Documents that were not labelled with the show title were labelled as "Consult Dr. Brothers" for consistency. The show was picked up by NBC and later ABC (1961). It ran from 1959 to circa 1961 and preceded "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers".
Sub-subseries VIIa.1. Files
Box 273
Index card file of show topics
 1959-08-10-1960-11-11
Box 39 Folder 82
Consult Dr. Brothers show topics
 1960-1962
Box 39 Folder 83
Dr. Joyce Brothers Program budget
 1962-10
Box 41 Folder 1
Notes and correspondence
 1961-1963
Scope and Contents
These notes likely relate to Consult Dr. Brothers, as they were found with related material, but may also contain information on "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers." Included are notes for redaction and censorship for the show from WEWS.
Box 41 Folder 2
Notes and correspondence
 1961-1965
Scope and Contents
These notes likely relate to Consult Dr. Brothers, as they were found with related material, but may also contain information on "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers."

 Undated
Scope and Contents
Includes publicity and publicity booklet from ABC Films.

Duplicate of the promos on V-12617, for channels 2-12, and general promo, filmed on March 25 ,1961. Followed by 30 minutes of blank tape, then 10 second promos for channels 2-12, and general promo. These promos were filmed on March 24, 1961.

Box 241 tr-19123
Consult Dr. Brothers
 undated, undated, 1959-04-14, 1959-04-15, 1959-04-16, 1959-04-17
Scope and Contents
Woman worried about past indescretion, man can't make up mind between two women, mothers possessive of sons in regards to marriage, feeding problem with child; woman curious about adoption, gang age in youngsters and stealing, marrying a younger man, how much can husband ask of wife?; rhinoplasty, woman worried about losing husband to younger neighbor, elderly mother's insomnia, children's fads; toilet training, why is wife hesitant about undressing in front of him, meddling grandmother, escaping reality; remarrying and the effect on children, prefectionist husband, why girlfriend has more dates but fewer friends?, problems that arise during first year of school; illegitimate child, granddaughter likes to break things
Box 241 tr-19123
Consult Dr. Brothers
 1959-04-02, 1959-04-03, 1959-04-07, 1959-04-08, 1959-04-09
Scope and Contents
What makes marriage work, social graces in the five year old, young love, teenage daughter doesn't make friends easily; husband making love to bridge partner, teenager has trouble getting along with brother and mother, young couple wants advice on controlling emotion; baby's schedule, question about sex life, how to stop smoking; how to stop smoking, in-law problem, woman thinks about death all the time, two children fighting all the time; woman thinks about death all the time, menopausal pregnancy, young woman losing fiance to younger girl, nine-year-old daughter jealous of nephew
Box 241 tr-19122
Consult Dr. Brothers
 1958-12-22-1958-12-29
Scope and Contents
Mother objecting to fiance, widow who remarries, wife taken for granted; woman lonely and bored, aging, boy who is shy; repeat letters: husband starting to look at other women, how much are you worth to your husband, woman with sexual problem; husband doesn't want to do anything after work, man feels he is not mature, how much are you worth to your husband; husband prefers dog over child, mother-in-law disrupting home, daydreaming and writing in a diary, children constantly fighting

Scope and Contents

Box 240 tr-19077
Consult Dr. Brothers
 1959-03-16-1959-03-20
Scope and Contents
Fun is missing in marriage, boys who become sissies, worried unborn child would have speech problems and how to prevent it, "at our age" conversations; college age men and maturity, waiting to fall madly in love for marriage; what traits a man likes in a woman, children are impossible when company comes, husband starting to wear women's clothing; children impossible when company comes, how to be a good grandmother, problems of married student; proper time to start toilet training, why are there so many unmarried women?, daydreaming stage in adolescence, flat-chested woman
Box 240 tr-19076
The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1959-08-31-1959-09-17
Scope and Contents
Excitement left marriage, ideal woman, transvestite, illegitimate sex, wife no orgasm, interfaith marriage, married students, romaantic seeking ideal, unmarried, flatchested, depressed women
Box 240 tr-19062
The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
1959-01-07, 1959-01-09, 1959-01-12, 1959-01-13, 1959-01-14
Scope and Contents
1959-01-07: daughter enjoys getting other children get in trouble, emotionally induced illness, inability to remember names and faces. 1959-01-09: husband wants night out with the boys, unwed mother. 1959-01-12: woman hits her baby too often, woman attracting men through telepathy, emotional needs of adolescents. 1959-01-13: husband locks wife out after quarrel, marriage in college, teenager is worried about dating, stuttering in child
Box 240 tr-19062
The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1958-12-30, 1958-12-31, 1959-01-02, 1959-01-05, 1959-01-06
Scope and Contents
1958-12-30: Pacifier problem, whether or not to marry jealous fiance, husband won't get a job. 1958-12-31: teenage son wants to date, father spoils children, stingy husband. 1959-01-02: father-in-law problem, not seeing fiance enough, husband is jealous of son. 1959-01-05: neighbor is invading privacy, avoiding reality, husband is coming home from prison, father-in-law problem. 1959-01-06: brother living with a married woman, popularity in teenagers, woman unable to have children-should she tell fiance?

The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1959-11-03-1959-11-23
Scope and Contents
Bored wife, divorcee anxiety and confession, husband infatuated with students, sex ed and nudist colony, trapped and jealous wife, unfaithful wife, women with homosexual dreams

The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1959-11-25-1959-12-15

Box 240 tr-19056
The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1959-10-14-1959-10-23
Box 240 tr-19057
The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show
 1959-08-12-1959-08-28
Scope and Contents
Shy working woman, teen girl with no dates, explaining death to son, husband's fear of flying, rape confession, woman alone, working woman, woman bored sexually, plus more
Subseries VIIb. "Dr. Joyce Brothers' Casebook"
Scope and Contents
Proposed program with Dr. Brothers dramatic presentation of cases based on actual letter. This show was 20-25 minutes. Brothers was represented by Lester Lewis Associates. This is a different show than "Consult Dr. Brothers"/"The Dr. Joyce Brothers show." It was not filmed and did not air, though a promo was shot.
Box 40 Folder 1
Contract for "Dr. Joyce Brothers Casebook" writers
 1960-02-18
Scope and Contents
Folder contains an unsigned contract between writer Stephen Gethers and Brothers (Lester Lewis Associates). Signed contracts with Adrian Spies, Steven Gethers

Sub-subseries VIIc.3. Films and Video
Processing Information
Brothers possessed multiple sets of films of Tell Me, Dr. Brothers. The sets were labelled A, B, and C, though many canisters were unlabelled. The best copy of each episode was digitized, regardless of which set it came from. The dates on original canisters do not match the date of filming, but may be air dates or dates that a film copy was made.

Scope and Contents
Segments and promos. Topics: mismatched couples, their psychological why and wherefore; rate yourself - what family secrets would your home reveal to a psychologist?; man passing 40; that teen age problem - party crashing; people who refuse to take medicine; baby bites; how to understand your husband; how to break up; not in front of the children.
Video 12648 V-12648
WFIL Episode 35
 1963-10-18
Scope and Contents
Segments and promos. Topics: if you dominate your husband; are women less honest than men on tax returns?; rate yourself - what social class do you belong to; how long a honeymoon; marriage after children; husband conerned about his looks; mother-in-law calle mother; adolescent grown; will two languages hurt your child.
Video 12623 V-12623
WFIL Episode 36
 1963-10-19
Scope and Contents
Promos and segments. Topics: marriage for money; daughters who commit suicide; man likes office; likes certain store; rate yourself: anxious; sweet tooth; togetherness doesn't make marriage; sons won't get into trouble (plus gifts)
Video 12642 V-12642
WFIL Episode 37
 1963-10-19
Scope and Contents
Segments and promos. Topics: that very misleading fallacy that divorce can be friendly; second childhood; conservative or cheap; husband qualities; rate yourself - are you aristocratic; your adolescent's undesirable friends; interesting facts you may not know about pain; when a father won't help with the baby.
Video 12620 V-12620
WFIL Episode 38
 1963-10-25
Scope and Contents
Some visual issues across screen. Promo at 19:30 severely distorted. Around 30 minute mark, a cut in the video picks up mid speech. Promos, segments, and kickers. Topics: unwed mothers; rate yourself: family secrets part 2; leading cause of worry; child naughty; man's fall and winter's blues; man to love woman; child stutter; misplaced sense of humor.
Video 12650 V-12650
WFIL Episode 39
 1963-10-25
Scope and Contents
Segments and promos. Topics: rate yourself - are you compulsive; attracts men who only want friendship; what's the motive behind the bargain hunters; when you're having your first baby; what men don't understand about women; youngster picked on by playmates.

The Strange Case of Dr. Brothers

Series X. Research Files
Subseries Xa. Card Files
Scope and Contents
Known as the "Tobies" (after Brothers' assistant), this card file consists of index cards with articles stapled to them. When asked to speak or right on a specific subject, Brothers' would pull from these cards as her references.
Processing Information
These cards are organized by alphabetically by topic, and original order has been maintained. These cards were originally housed in 16 filing cabinets.

Series XI. "Shorties"
Scope and Contents
This series contains numbered articles and segments, arranged by Brothers. She referred to them "Shorties."
Processing Information
Originally housed in filing cabinets. Removed and rearranged as necessary to keep numeric order.
